title: Survival Basics
intro: Vim is modal. In Normal mode the keys `h j k l` move the cursor left, down, up, and right. Press `i` to enter Insert mode and type text, then press `Esc` to return to Normal mode. Delete the character under the cursor with `x`.

exercises.instruction: Move down to the TODO comment on line 3 using `j`.
hints: Each press of `j` moves the cursor down one line.

exercises.instruction: Move right until the cursor sits on the `v` of `value` using `l`.
hints: `l` moves one character to the right, `h` moves back left.

exercises.instruction: Move up to the first line using `k`.
hints: Each press of `k` moves the cursor up one line.

exercises.instruction: There is a stray `X` in the middle of `hello`. The cursor is already on it — delete it with `x`.
hints: `x` deletes the character under the cursor.

exercises.instruction: The keyword should be `const`, not `cnst`. The cursor is on the `n` — press `i` to insert, type the missing `o`, then press `Esc`.
hints: `i` inserts text before the cursor. Don't forget `Esc` to leave Insert mode.
